sentence: O tempu nãu ehtá nada bom agora.
ipa: o ˈtẽpu ˈnɐ̃w̃ eˈhta ˈnadɐ ˈbõ ɐˈɡɔɾɐ
gloss_en: The weather is not good at all right now.
notes: Convenção orthography throughout (final -o written <u>, aspirated coda <h>, nasal diphthong written <ãu>) per convencao_barranquenho2025; <nãu> adapted by the regular <ãu> rule (Dicionário -ãu headwords, e.g. <alabãu>/<aldeãu>, per dicionario_barranquenho2025); coda-/s/ aspiration [h] in <ehtá> per clements_amaral_luis2008; attested <agora> per navas2011.

sentence: Ehtes çapatus custam catorze eurus.
ipa: ˈehtɨ sɐˈpatu ˈkuhtɐ̃w̃ kɐˈtoɾzɨ ˈewɾu
gloss_en: These shoes cost fourteen euros.
notes: Attested <catorze> [kɐˈtɔɾzɨ]-type per navas2011; coda aspiration [h] and absolute-final /s/ deletion (written plural -s stays silent) per convencao_barranquenho2025 and clements_amaral_luis2008.

sentence: A reuniãu é amanhã aqui na bila.
ipa: ɐ reˈwniɐ̃w̃ ˈɛ ɐmɐˈɲã ɐˈki nɐ ˈbilɐ
gloss_en: The meeting is tomorrow here in the village.
notes: <bila> written with <b> (betacism is orthographic: the Dicionário headword list has no <v> spellings, per dicionario_barranquenho2025 and gramatica_barranquenho2025; adapted by rule, lemma itself not a headword); <reuniãu> adapted by the <ãu> rule per convencao_barranquenho2025; attested <aqui> [ɐˈki] per navas2011; nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] per convencao_barranquenho2025.

sentence: O meu irmãu é pedreiro e bibe aqui.
ipa: o ˈmew iˈɾmɐ̃w̃ ˈɛ pɨˈdɾejɾu i ˈbibɨ ɐˈki
gloss_en: My brother is a bricklayer and lives here.
notes: Attested <pedreiro> and <aqui> per navas2011; <bibe> adapted-by-rule betacism spelling (Dicionário attests the final -i variant <bibi>, per dicionario_barranquenho2025; final <-e> = [ɨ] kept per convencao_barranquenho2025); <irmãu> adapted by the <ãu> rule (the Dicionário headword <ermáu> is a distinct vocalism, not this utterance) per dicionario_barranquenho2025; betacism per gramatica_barranquenho2025.

sentence: Bira à direita e segue pur ehta rua.
ipa: ˈbiɾɐ a diˈɾejtɐ i ˈsɛɡɨ ˈpuɾ ˈehtɐ ˈruɐ
gloss_en: Turn right and continue along this street.
notes: <Bira> adapted-by-rule betacism spelling per gramatica_barranquenho2025 and dicionario_barranquenho2025 (no <v> headwords); monosyllabic <pur> keeps its /r/ (word-exception) and initial trill [r] in <rua> per convencao_barranquenho2025; coda aspiration per clements_amaral_luis2008.

sentence: A sopa de tomate ehtava mehmu boa.
ipa: ɐ ˈsopɐ dɨ tuˈmat eˈhtabɐ ˈmɛhmu ˈboɐ
gloss_en: The tomato soup was really good.
notes: Attested headword <mehmu> [ˈmɛhmu] per dicionario_barranquenho2025 (coda-/s/ aspiration written <h>, final -o written <u>); coda-/s/ aspiration in <ehtava> per navas2011 and clements_amaral_luis2008; alveolar (non-chiado) sibilants per gramatica_barranquenho2025.

sentence: Onde é que fica a casa da tua abó?
ipa: ˈõdɨ ˈɛ kɨ ˈfikɐ ɐ ˈkazɐ dɐ ˈtuɐ ɐˈbɔ
gloss_en: Where is your grandmother's house?
notes: Attested headword <abó> [ɐˈbɔ] (betacism written <b>) per dicionario_barranquenho2025 and navas2011; plain alveolar [z] in <casa> (no chiado, seseo system) per gramatica_barranquenho2025; coda-nasal vowel [õ] in <onde> per convencao_barranquenho2025.

sentence: Cuantas pessoas bên à matança do porcu?
ipa: ˈkuɐ̃tɐ ˈpɛs̺uɐ ˈbẽ a mɐˈtɐ̃sɐ du ˈpɔɾku
gloss_en: How many people are coming to the pig slaughter?
notes: Alentejo rural register (matança do porco) per navas2011; <Cuantas> adapted by the Dicionário <cua> pattern (<cualquera>, <cuartu>, per dicionario_barranquenho2025) — engine reads the sequence as hiatus [ku.ɐ̃] (rising glide unmodelled), a documented engine limitation; <bên> adapted-by-rule betacism + word-final nasal <-n> (cf. attested <quen>, per dicionario_barranquenho2025); <porcu> final -o written <u> per convencao_barranquenho2025; absolute-final /s/ deletion in <cuantas>/<pessoas> per clements_amaral_luis2008.

sentence: Se eu pudesse, compraba uma casa bonita.
ipa: sɨ ˈew puˈdɛs̺ɨ kõˈpɾabɐ ˈumɐ ˈkazɐ buˈnitɐ
gloss_en: If I could, I would buy a pretty house.
notes: Attested <bonita> per navas2011; <compraba> adapted-by-rule betacism spelling (no <v> headwords, per dicionario_barranquenho2025) [kõˈpɾabɐ] per gramatica_barranquenho2025; alveolar sibilants per convencao_barranquenho2025.

sentence: Comprámos pãu e binhu na feira de Barrancus.
ipa: kõˈpɾamu ˈpɐ̃w̃ i ˈbiɲu nɐ ˈfejɾɐ dɨ bɐˈrɐ̃ku
gloss_en: We bought bread and wine at the Barrancos fair.
notes: Kept <ei> diphthong in <feira> (variable retention, kept variant attested in <sei>) per navas2011; attested headword <binhu> per dicionario_barranquenho2025; <pãu> by the <ãu> rule and <Barrancus> plural -us (cf. <çapatus>/<eurus>) per convencao_barranquenho2025; initial trill [r]-type <rr> per convencao_barranquenho2025.

sentence: Amanhã bamos bê os meus currais no campu.
ipa: ɐmɐˈɲã ˈbamu ˈbe o ˈmew kuˈraj nu ˈkɐ̃pu
gloss_en: Tomorrow we go to see my corrals in the field.
notes: Attested phrase <os meus currais> [ˈo ˈmew ˈkuraj] with final-/s/ deletion per navas2011; <bamos> adapted-by-rule betacism; <bê> is the Dicionário-style spelling of 'ver' (tonic final /r/ deletion written with circumflex, cf. <comê>/<dí>, per dicionario_barranquenho2025) — engine yields close-mid [ˈbe], replacing the earlier spelling-artifact open [ˈbɛ]; <campu> and coda-nasal [ɐ̃] per convencao_barranquenho2025 and clements_amaral_luis2008; <currais> is oxytone [kuˈraj] (word-final oral diphthong ⟨ai⟩ = tonic final, regular Portuguese aguda rule).

sentence: Telefona à tua madre cuandu puderes.
ipa: tɨlɨˈfɔnɐ a ˈtuɐ ˈmadɾɨ ˈkuɐ̃du puˈdɛɾɨ
gloss_en: Call your mother when you can.
notes: Contact lexicon <madre> (Spanish adstrate) per navas2011; <cuandu> adapted by the Dicionário <cua> pattern + final -u (cf. <cualquera>/<cuartu>, per dicionario_barranquenho2025) — engine reads hiatus [ku.ɐ̃] (rising glide unmodelled); absolute-final /s/ deletion in <puderes> per clements_amaral_luis2008; nasal [ɐ̃] per convencao_barranquenho2025.

sentence: Bom dia, como fazes de noite no berãu?
ipa: ˈbõ ˈdiɐ ˈkomu ˈfazɨ dɨ ˈnojtɨ nu bɨˈɾɐ̃w̃
gloss_en: Good morning, how do you spend the night in summer?
notes: Attested sentence <como fazes de noite> per navas2011; <berãu> adapted-by-rule betacism + <ãu> spelling [bɨˈɾɐ̃w̃] per gramatica_barranquenho2025 and convencao_barranquenho2025; final-/s/ deletion in <fazes> per clements_amaral_luis2008.

sentence: Mais bale um pássaru na mãu que dois a boá.
ipa: ˈmaj ˈbalɨ ˈũ ˈpas̺ɐɾu nɐ ˈmɐ̃w̃ kɨ ˈdoj ɐ ˈbɔa
gloss_en: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
notes: Proverb with adapted-by-rule betacism <bale>/<boá> [ˈbalɨ]/[ˈbɔa] (tonic final /r/ deletion written with acute, cf. <abanadó>, per dicionario_barranquenho2025) per gramatica_barranquenho2025 and clements_amaral_luis2008; <pássaru> keeps the proparoxytone written accent with final -o written <u> per convencao_barranquenho2025 (the accent places the engine stress on the initial syllable, matching the attested proparoxytone); <mãu> by the <ãu> rule; final-/s/ deletion in <mais>/<dois> per navas2011.

sentence: O gatu pretu dorme na coluna do pátiu.
ipa: o ˈɡatu ˈpɾɛtu ˈdɔɾmɨ nɐ kuˈlunɐ du ˈpatiw
gloss_en: The black cat sleeps by the column of the patio.
notes: Attested <coluna> [kuˈlunɐ] per navas2011 and attested headword <gatu> per dicionario_barranquenho2025; <pretu>/<pátiu> final -o written <u> per convencao_barranquenho2025 — in <pátiu> the written -u surfaces as an offglide [ˈpatiw]; retained EP-type unstressed reduction (pretonic <o>->[u]) per navas2011.

sentence: Aquele ratu fugiu agora para o buracu.
ipa: ɐˈkɛlɨ ˈratu fuˈʒiw ɐˈɡɔɾɐ ˈpaɾɐ o buˈɾaku
gloss_en: That mouse fled just now into the hole.
notes: Attested <agora> per navas2011; <ratu>/<buracu> final -o written <u> per convencao_barranquenho2025; word-initial alveolar trill [r] in <ratu> (not EP uvular) per convencao_barranquenho2025 and gramatica_barranquenho2025; <fugiu> is oxytone [fuˈʒiw] (word-final oral diphthong ⟨iu⟩ = tonic final).

sentence: O só brilha sobre o campu e o monte.
ipa: o ˈsɔ ˈbɾiʎɐ ˈsobɾ o ˈkɐ̃pu i o ˈmõtɨ
gloss_en: The sun shines over the field and the hill.
notes: Tonic final /l/ deletion written (<só> for 'sol', cf. attested <mé> for 'mel', per dicionario_barranquenho2025) [ˈsɔ] per clements_amaral_luis2008 and convencao_barranquenho2025; palatal [ʎ] in <brilha> and coda-nasal [ɐ̃ õ] per gramatica_barranquenho2025; <campu> final -u per convencao_barranquenho2025.

sentence: Eu les pedia sempre um poucu de mé.
ipa: ˈew ˈlɛ ˈpɛdiɐ ˈsẽpɾɨ ˈũ ˈpoku dɨ ˈmɛ
gloss_en: I always asked them for a bit of honey.
notes: Attested contact clitic <les> (Spanish adstrate) in <eu les pedia> per navas2011; Convenção final -o written <u> in <poucu> and attested headword <mé> ('mel', tonic final /l/ deletion written) [ˈmɛ] per convencao_barranquenho2025 and dicionario_barranquenho2025.

sentence: Eu sei que o rio corre mal no berãu.
ipa: ˈew ˈsej k o ˈriu ˈkɔrɨ ˈmal nu bɨˈɾɐ̃w̃
gloss_en: I know the river runs poorly in summer.
notes: Attested <sei> [sej] with kept <ei> per navas2011; monosyllable <mal> keeps its /l/ (word-exception, Convenção §2.6 Nota) and trills in <rio>/<corre> per convencao_barranquenho2025; <berãu> adapted-by-rule betacism + <ãu> spelling per gramatica_barranquenho2025.

sentence: A minha filha canta uma cançãu bonita.
ipa: ɐ ˈmiɲɐ ˈfiʎɐ ˈkɐ̃tɐ ˈumɐ kɐ̃ˈsɐ̃w̃ buˈnitɐ
gloss_en: My daughter sings a pretty song.
notes: Attested <bonita> per navas2011; <cançãu> adapted by the <ãu> rule (cf. Dicionário <atensãu>, per dicionario_barranquenho2025); palatals [ɲ ʎ] in <minha>/<filha> and nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] per gramatica_barranquenho2025 and convencao_barranquenho2025.
